div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in China (Johnson)
div.p:
103. Your 191, June 30, 1 p.m. The Department authorizes the Embassy to make informally appropriate representations to the Chinese Foreign Office in regard to the adverse effects, on the business of
the interested American firms, of smuggling into North China of kerosene and gasoline, but feels that the Embassy should avoid making a specific suggestion that a preventive office be established at the Tientsin East Station. However, the Department perceives no objection to the making of such a suggestion by the companies themselves should they desire to do so.
